labels.controls_search_hint_agendas: Searches title, description, number and category.
labels.controls_search_hint_alignment: Searches the name of the compared member.
labels.controls_search_hint_memberships: Searches group/body name, function and membership type.
labels.controls_search_hint_interests: Searches organisation name, function and parliamentary group.
labels.controls_search_hint_votes: Searches the voting title and the affair title.
labels.controls_search_hint_votings: Searches the voting title and the affair title.
labels.controls_search_hint_affairs: Searches affair title and affair number.
labels.voting_chart_alt: Seat map of {count} members, coloured by {mode}. {summary}
labels.voting_table_alt: Table of how {count} members voted, one row per member, with their party and parliamentary group.
labels.voting_chart_seat_note: Seat positions are illustrative, not the real chamber layout. Open the editor to rearrange the colours, labels and order, and export.
labels.topics_intro: The words this person uses most across their speeches, with common filler words removed. Tile size shows how often a word is used.
labels.topics_alt: Treemap of the {count} most-used words, sized by how often each is used. {ctx}
labels.topics_table_alt: Table of the {count} most-used words and how often each is used. {ctx}
labels.topics_empty: No speeches with usable text in this period. Try widening the range.
labels.loyalty_subtitle: Share of a member's votes cast against their group's majority line (higher = more independent). Abstentions count as a position.
labels.loyalty_axis: Dissent rate: share of votes against own group
labels.loyalty_empty: No votes in this period meet the thresholds for a loyalty score. Widen the range.
labels.loyalty_chart_alt: Beeswarm plot of the dissent rate of {count} members, one dot per member, coloured by party. {ctx}
labels.loyalty_table_alt: Table of {count} members with their votes, dissents and dissent rate. {ctx}
labels.no_alignment: Not enough shared votes to compare.
labels.body_alignment_subtitle: How members of this chamber vote relative to one another, from the votes they have in common. In the map, members who vote alike sit close together and opponents far apart. Colours are parties, so blocs and coalitions show up as clusters. The grid gives the average agreement between each pair of parties (the diagonal is each party's own internal cohesion).
labels.alignment_empty: Not enough votes in this window to build the model.
labels.alignment_window_note: Most meaningful within a single legislative period: the model compares members from the votes they have in common. If the window spans an election or a change in composition, members with different terms barely overlap and positions become less comparable.
labels.alignment_no_parties: No party groups to compare in this window.
labels.alignment_scatter_alt: Scatter plot of {count} members placed by how similarly they voted, coloured by party. {ctx}
labels.alignment_heatmap_note: Each cell is the average agreement between members of the two parties (diagonal = a party's internal cohesion). ± is the 95% confidence range and n the number of member-pairs behind it. A small range over many pairs means the alignment is well-established; a wide range means it's only suggestive.
labels.alignment_heatmap_alt: Grid of the average voting agreement between {count} parties. {ctx}
labels.alignment_members_table_alt: Table of {count} members with their agreement with the chamber and with their own party. {ctx}
labels.lobby_subtitle: The organizations members of this chamber declare in their register of interests, and where those ties overlap. In the network, members are dots (coloured by party) and organizations shared by two or more members are squares. Members who share the same organizations pull together. The breakdown shows how many mandates are paid versus unpaid, and who holds the most.
labels.lobby_empty: No declared interests for this chamber in this window.
labels.lobby_no_shared: No organizations are shared by two or more members here. See the breakdown.
labels.lobby_network_alt: Network graph of {members} members and the {orgs} organizations they share, linked by declared interests. {ctx}
labels.discussion_intro: Where members sit on a latent axis derived purely from the words they use in their speeches (Wordfish scaling).
labels.discussion_error: The speech scaling could not be computed.
labels.discussion_not_converged: Model did not fully converge. Read positions with extra caution.
labels.discussion_plot_alt: Beeswarm plot of {count} members placed along a word-choice axis, one dot per member. {ctx}
labels.discussion_plot_caption: Each dot is one member, placed by the words they use in their speeches. Members who reach for the same distinctive words sit close together; members who talk very differently sit far apart. So a member's spot isn't assigned by us; it comes out of how they actually speak. For example, someone who keeps saying “tax”, “budget” and “spending” lands far from someone who mostly says “asylum”, “border” and “integration”. Hover any dot for its details. Filled dots have enough speech to place reliably; hollow rings spoke too little, so read their position with caution.
labels.discussion_empty: No speeches are available for this parliament, so its members can't be placed on the axis. This scaling reads the words members actually speak, and the data set only holds speech transcripts for a few parliaments: the federal chambers and a handful of cantons such as Bern, Basel-Stadt or St. Gallen. You can also try a longer time range above.
labels.discussion_empty_sparse: There is too little speech transcript text for this parliament to place its members on the axis. Try a longer time range above.
labels.discussion_align_hint: How much of the spread in positions is explained by party membership (η²): 100% = parties are perfectly separated, ~0% = party tells you nothing.
labels.discussion_poles_title: What the axis is made of
labels.discussion_poles_hint: The words that most pull members towards each pole. The axis only means something once you read these. Its direction is arbitrary.
labels.discussion_table_alt: Table of {count} members with their position on the word-choice axis. {ctx}
labels.discussion_read_direction: The axis direction is arbitrary: Wordfish finds the split, not which side is “left” or “right”. Read the word poles to see what each side means.
labels.discussion_read_uncertainty: Positions are standardized, so the spread is a convention, not a real distance. Wider error bars (and hollow dots) mean less certainty, so don't over-read those members.
labels.discussion_read_confound: Speech style reflects role as much as ideology: committee chairs, ministers and frequent speakers can land at a pole because of what they do, not only what they believe.
labels.org_members_table_alt: Table of {count} members with their party, body, role, payment and period. {ctx}
labels.lobby_table_explain: The full matrix marks every member against every shared organization: one dot per declared tie, the exhaustive view of who is tied to what.
labels.lobby_table_alt: Table marking each of {count} members against the {orgs} organizations they are tied to, one dot per declared tie. {ctx}
labels.lobby_select_hint: Select a member or organization to see its ties.
labels.lobby_error: The lobby network could not be computed.
labels.not_found_hint: There is no entry with the ID {id}. It may have been removed, or the link may be outdated.
